content: Would caecus, goliath, tulu, miryam work for a story team?
Which teams do you guys recommend and which ones I should focus on level for now?

I'm also a beginner, but from what I see from your roster, I think you could pull off pure Caro team with Thais, Helot, Aigis and Faint. Because Thais give so much embryo so go around pure Caro team. 

Please correct me if I'm wrong
Pure tentales
is usually good
Thais is a good support
I couldnt tell you how well it scaled
but Thais Faint has decent survivability
she also has a way to set up vuln for Aigis so yeah pure caro could work

Ogier, Aigis (or Thais), Ramona and Helot

Celeste, Aurita, Tinct and Jenkin

It would be easy to recommend Helot as a pure Caro team, but in my experience Helot can be a bit fussy about power.

Ogier helps as a tank and as a support for Helot, and I recommend that you use Ogier's Posse on that team. 

Ramona is a great help because Helot relies entirely on her 4 damage cost, and with Ramona as a companion, she can have a powerful second, or even third, hit at your disposal.

And Aigis is Aigis. Thais isn't a bad idea either. Try both teams and stick with the one you're most comfortable with
Sanga, Karen or Nautilus wouldn't do better than Ogier?
Any suggestions for team? I’ve been kinda just slapping random characters together

The catch with all of them is that they don't buff the team. Sanga provides tentacles to the team, and her Exalt's damage is based on the team's shield count. This isn't something Helot seeks out.

Karen heals the team, yes, and also helps with arithmetic, but it's unnecessary when you learn to play with Helot-Ramona.

And Nautila load the aliemus a little to his companions and provides shields. But it's not something Helot is looking for.

Ogier's Exalt provides strength bonuses and with his wheel equipped he makes it even more so

Here I'll share an example of what can be achieved with a team, although it may not be the best example. 

I hope that illustrates my point well. <:smh:1356053161999335514>

Karen, Caecus, Aurita and 24

Caecus, Ramona, Nautila and Pandia
What team is good for doresain?
24, ogier, thais.
I haven't tested how well the team would work with thais replaces via an another baby maker... since... her ult like... one in a million to get the gdamn dorsain 3 cost.
I’m just starting chapter 2 now, what’s a good team I could run? I still have 4 of the protoparadise unit selectors too

Ogier, Aigis, Ramona and Helot 

Faint, Aigis, Goliath and Uvhash

Doll (although you better get Karen), Nautila, Pandia and Daffodil (you must play her as a counter, using her cost 2 from Exalt)
To build around goliath, caecus, tulu tentacles for story clearing, would thais or miryam be a better support?
I like miryam's additional tentacles, but getting the caro mechanics included for health and ult gain has been pretty nice

i've got a few teams working here depending on what dps i want to use, but what should my priorities for picking up new characters/wheels  be here and how should i use my protoparadise/protoype horizons? Currently i am pulling for Thais's e2, but after i get that, I'm less certain about what I should get. Should I get her wheel? Or should i get doresain to use in my ghelot team? 

Also i was thinking about using protoparadise on jenkin but i have daff and tawil, and they feel better than jenkins in a lot of ways. And i'm not sure if i need any other standard characters for my current teams. I'm also undecided about how to allot my protoype horizons, I have 4 right now. i was thinking like 2 on helot and 2 on tawil. idk though.

My current go to teams are Jenkin, tulu, murphy, corpo
My new team is gHelot, thais, Corpo, Tulu/murphy? or like ultra with winkle and aigis
Yeah. Catena, Thais, Aigis and Winkle sound better

Spend your normal pulls on maxing Titan's Blade (Goliath's WoD, whatever the hell it's named now)
Your D-Jewel project character being Aurita is great if you want to use her with Tulu, Murphy, and Corposant. Jenkin is a great character, but your team composition will suffer for having her, since she doesn't combine with these units well (You can't really do Ultra/Caro because you'd only have Jenkin, Thais, and Winkle; you could do Aequor/Ultra, but running Jenkin seems like it would be strictly worse than Tulu/Aurita/Corposant/Winkle or Tulu/Corposant/Murphy/Winkle...)
I threw a few months into GHelot in order to get the overexalt, which seems to become an assumption the game makes of you after you start getting into lv65~ content. Getting the overexalt stage in general is a shockingly good boost in strength and severely limits the negative impact of anything that lowers your aliemus (Symptom: Depression, Festival of Tides, fighting Goliath in general, etc.) and it takes a long ass time so you might as well get moving on it earlier rather than later

You're at the point where Goliath will cease to be a functional primary damage unit, so I would focus your protoparadise into gHelot or Tulu. Aurita and Jenkin already get merges from the store, so you won't be hard walled with them the same way you will be with the limited and semi-limited characters.
24/Miryam/Gdoll/Lily is the best posion comp in game atm?
probably, shit's nuts
Ive been using it as my main team for a while now and honestly sweating a lot harder than I expected with it but idk if im just taking on content too high level for me or the team isnt that strong.
What level/chapter
I hit account level 55 today and ive been having a hard time in 2nd half of chapter 8. Im clearing it but sometimes I gotta use gnosis once, even twice
sounds about right
Okay so its just normal to struggle this much
8-9 is just Like That
Gotcha
8-9 is where I abandoned my pride as a man's man and started using the emergency supplies, but there was another way
The especially cursed power of abandoning your shiny SSR wheels and simply sacrificing one character to carry Frenzy and Gluttony
Guys newcomer here I got caecus who should I aim next? And what team mate do he function best with
pull all blue
can't go wrong
I mean that literally, none of the Aequor characters are bad and they all work together even if they're doing different stuff
8-9 felt so overtuned compared to everything ive fough previously
Rejoice. It gets worse.
I thought since I invested so much into dupes for this team that my time would be easy
Unfortunately, it's hard to actually hit the intended target with any of your attacks, so actually building the poison to begin with is a massive pain in the ass
That and starting a fight (normal battle, not even the boss stage) and its first attack hits harder than my total hp. I wonder sometimes
https://tenor.com/view/munsonated-gif-26549705
The 8-12 elite battle reliably triggered my death resistance and made me change my team composition from my pure Caro preference
As it turns out, simply pretending to yourself that Leigh is good enough will not make it so
I actually loved her design. I picked her from the first multi that you can reroll when you start the game. Only to later look up a tier list and realize my blunder
I got current on content before I looked at a tier list

I finally completed the Chains Unbound WoD not even a month prior and saw the guy's recommendation on WoDs; a lot of his insight on stuff was well reasoned and I couldn't really argue with it, so I got to that one and his argument for Blade of the Titan was "If anyone tells you this isn't the best standard wheel to run on your DPS they simply do not understand the game" and I was like "Shit he's right"

What was worse, literally an hour before I read that, I had already used the wheel reset the patch had given me to get rid of Nymphaea's wheel again, so I couldn't break down Chains Unbound to max out Blade of the Titan
So I'm just sitting here increasingly pissed off every day over not having thought of it already
https://tenor.com/view/xqc-xqcl-gif-25519231

Usually you look at a tier list to determine the best course of action if you have access to all of the options. Given that this is a gacha, I waited until I had most of the characters before considering the concept of alternatives
Luckily I found out about Titan blade when I started so never stopped pulling on it. 2 copies away from maxing it out
I have to say, I really find it appropriate that Helot Catena can use Drowning in Crimson and Chains Unbound pretty freely, so while Blade of the Titan is objectively numerically superior
I currently have +124.4% damage dealt, so this isn't a *bad* choice
I'm just not breaking 300k damage every time I ult regardless of the state of my hand
I picked Gelot from that scam banner few weeks ago and managed to get her and her wheel. Probably gonna work on her as my 2nd team when im done with story. What teammates do you recommend for her?
GDoll, if she can overexalt; otherwise Horla and Thais
I ran gHelot, Leigh, Faint, and Aigis for a long ass time before I had to give up on it
Is there a benefit in running mono of a realm instead of mixing it?
Yes, but not enough of one, in the case of Horla
Your core mechanic works better, and in Caro, that means the crimson furnace filling up and shield you get from Devour work more efficiently
I see, whats the benefit if you run mono in blue realm?
The realm mastery scaling of the tranquil sea shield and the damage done by raging waves is increased, and you get another tentacle at the start of combat
I see, maybe if Gdoll wasnt so broken it might have been worth running full blue
By "increased," we're talking about decimal points on a percentage
And is generally only relevant if you actually try to scale realm mastery, which... shouldn't naturally happen

Characters who are cursed to carry realm mastery like Leigh or Celeste generally suffer from it because they don't scale with any more statistically significant attribute like SKE, and while it is funny to get the tentacle slaps to give you five shield per hit, I can get into matches where I have well over three thousand max health
And the partial Chaos bonus to Aequor is "Aequor characters gain +10 Aliemus when Chaos characters ult," so no, gDoll is not only goated, but also activates the silly mechanic
Glad you  told me that the realm mastery for blue isnt that great since I watched a guide earlier today about blue realm and the video suggested to go all out on realm mastery for better tentacles

As a consequence of the fact that aequor's tentacle shield scales off of your maximum health more than it does off of realm mastery, using a character with a high base constitution and scaling the stat they actually care about is significantly more important than running Celeste and stacking realm mastery on her because she can't really do anything other than jump a teamwide substat
Thats why goliath is so good? Since I heard his stats is crazy
Goliath is good because he steals all strength from all enemies when it's time to combo off

which means E2 Goliath is a functional character *as a support,* and subsequently, only in specific situations (ie, Helot just gained one thousand strength, and is about to suffer a great fall down some stairs)
Okay so he is decent but not good enough to warrant switching out my E3 lily for him? Since ive considered that before
In the poison team? God no
but I love lily too much to get myself to do it
happy days
He's a great situational counter but I can't imagine you'd particularly need it for them
Would Hameln be better than Lily then for that comp?
no clue what he does, just saw he was in most poison comps ive checked online
for example on the poison team sugggestion on flares spreadsheet
Hameln is too good to not include in a hypothetical team composition regardless of what that team composition is because Hameln lets you play more of the game than normal
Playing more of the game than normal is generally a critical part of breaking it the fuck in half, so
Damn, hope he gets a rerun then soon so that I can whale for him
He's just that kind of character; he stuffs the deck full of Insight for free and that's never bad

The other part of the problem is their function is different; Lily engages with enemies using a defensive/recovery method, and will occasionally inflict massive damage with Strike to Protect, which naturally dovetails with a poison team by virtue of triggering the poison after the cost goes down from Prepare. However, this means you will 
- Draw Strike to Protect
- Wait more than one turn for it to go down in cost, then play it

By comparison, Hameln accelerates basically every style of play that isn't dependent on passing your turn and getting your shit rocked by a basic enemy that does 900 damage, and past lv60, you start getting into combats where defending yourself is significantly less attractive than simply killing the enemy immediately, therefore Hameln takes priority over Lily
That makes sense, so would he be good enough to replace lily at base copy or would he only be good enough to replace an E3 lily after a certain dupe milestone?
Hard for me to say
I don't use Hameln or Lily a lot, and also don't use the poison comp
feels like he would just work though
Honestly people did warn me that the poison comp isnt worth it but I was in too deep already
It actually gets significantly better in the second arc, but
At higher levels, the entire game turns into rocket tag, which poison isn't naturally good at
If you're scrambling every turn to avoid getting turned into a red smear by literally everything you run across, poisoning it and waiting for it to die is *way* harder than simply killing it

Fortunately, 24 and gDoll are actually pretty good at simply killing the enemy in front of them, Stellar Chapters have relics that forcibly trigger poison once it's inflicted on enemies as well as having multiplicative increases to Poison inflicted from a source of damage, and there are enemies that are uniquely susceptible to being killed by DoT mechanics, so it's not a total loss
Honestly I kill enemies more with just the dmg from 24 than actually killing them with poison which was very confusing
~~You're not completely fucked like you would be if you were using Nymphaea, Agrippa, or Liz~~
24 is a character that works a lot like Uvhash
Spend meter like water and massacre everything in your field of view
Does those two works together? And what other unit I could put along them?

Ahhhh, Sanga. Fully defensive tho. From what others told me, if you want Sanga (with over exalt) to be full force offensive with the exalt, you're gonna need Miryam, Alva, and Nautila. 

For me, I'm still using Caecus, Alva and Nautila with her since I got no Miryam yet tho
I pair Goliath usually with Tulu ngl. Idk any way to optimize Goliath properly because I need the arithmetica after exalt. Then there's the tricky part of boosting power
I don't think they're that good together tho
Like Sanga and Goliath
Thank u!
Yes that team is good. Bad for chapter 8 though because you don't have a good card to abuse the chapter gimmick.

Don't think you should ever replace an E3 Lily in a poison comp.
Also Hameln felt a bit too slow in Arc 2. Fights do too much damage to let you rouse a non-dps first.
And without rouse, Hameln has difficulty getting exalt so you are only getting 2 insights out of his kit.
